seoTitle: Add a Table of Contents in Confluence (2026 Guide)
metaDescription: Learn how to add an auto-updating table of contents in Confluence using the 2026 TOC macro, filter heading levels, and style the outline before publishing.
intro: Long technical pages become hard to navigate without a clear outline. This guide shows how to add a self-updating table of contents to a Confluence page in 2026, so readers can jump straight to the section they need.

steps.heading: Open the TOC macro
steps.body: In edit mode, click your insertion point and type `/toc` or `/table of contents` to deploy the automated index block.

steps.heading: Open macro settings
steps.body: Click the inserted macro container to open the floating advanced options panel.

steps.heading: Set heading depth
steps.body: Configure the minimum and maximum heading levels you want the outline to track.

steps.heading: Choose a display style
steps.body: Select between bulleted lists or a flat index format, and exclude any terms you don't want listed.

steps.heading: Verify your headings
steps.body: Check the sidebar panel to confirm your document's heading hierarchy looks correct before publishing.

steps.heading: Publish the page
steps.body: Click Publish to make the dynamic table of contents live.

faq.question: Does the table of contents update automatically as I edit the page?
faq.answer: Yes, the TOC macro scans active heading levels in real time and updates itself as you edit text.

faq.question: Can I limit the table of contents to certain heading levels?
faq.answer: Yes, the macro's depth controls let you isolate specific tiers, from Heading 1 down to detailed sub-headings.

faq.question: Why do my table of contents links not jump to the right spot?
faq.answer: Broken jump links are usually fixed by structuring proper paragraph blocks around headings so the index snaps to the correct location.

faq.question: Can I put the table of contents in a sidebar instead of the top of the page?
faq.answer: Yes, you can embed the outline inside a fixed right-hand panel or column wrapper for full-page scannability.
